question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of `runningHash` integrity verification in `poll()` so that tampered `TopicMessage` records injected into the mirror node database are served as authentic Hashgraph history, causing subscribers to receive falsified consensus messages with no detectable anomaly?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `poll()` never validates `sequenceNumber` continuity across successive pages, so that a gap or duplication in `sequenceNumber` values in the underlying store is silently streamed to subscribers as valid Hashgraph history, allowing history manipulation to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user cause `onNext()` to advance the `last` AtomicReference to a tampered `TopicMessage` with a forged `consensusTimestamp`, so that subsequent calls to `poll()` use `last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1` as the new `startTime`, permanently skipping legitimate historical messages and delivering an incomplete Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `startTime` derivation `last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1` in `poll()` where `consensusTimestamp` is a `long`, causing a long overflow if a tampered record with `Long.MAX_VALUE` as `consensusTimestamp` is inserted, resulting in a negative `startTime` that causes the query to replay the entire Hashgraph history from the beginning?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `isComplete()` logic that compares `pageSize.get() < retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize()` to cause premature termination of history retrieval by injecting a tampered page boundary, so that the subscriber never receives the full Hashgraph message history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, Duration.ofSeconds(1))` in `retrieve()` to keep a polling session alive indefinitely against a tampered topic, continuously re-reading manipulated Hashgraph history and amplifying the impact of injected false records across all retry cycles?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of `runningHashVersion` validation in `poll()` so that a tampered `TopicMessage` with a downgraded `runningHashVersion` (e.g., version 1 instead of 3) is served to subscribers, causing clients that rely on the hash version to apply the wrong verification algorithm and accept falsified Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `poll()` constructs a new filter via `filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build()` without re-validating `startTime` against the current consensus time, so that a manipulated `last.consensusTimestamp` causes the new filter to query future timestamps, returning empty pages and making the subscriber believe the Hashgraph history has ended prematurely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: PollingContext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `throttled=false` path in `PollingContext()` where `numRepeats = unthrottled.getMaxPolls()` is finite, so that by injecting tampered records that cause each poll to return a full page, the retriever exhausts `maxPolls` before delivering all legitimate history, causing a truncated and manipulated view of Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `limit` calculation `(int)(filter.getLimit() - context.getTotal().get())` in `poll()` where the cast to `int` can overflow if `total` exceeds `Integer.MAX_VALUE`, causing a negative page size that corrupts the query and delivers manipulated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: onNext()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the non-atomic update sequence in `onNext()` where `last.set(topicMessage)`, `total.incrementAndGet()`, and `pageSize.incrementAndGet()` are three separate operations, so that a concurrent poll triggered by the reactive scheduler observes an inconsistent state where `last` points to a tampered message but `total` has not yet been incremented, causing incorrect limit enforcement and history manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `timeout(retrieverProperties.getTimeout(), scheduler)` in `retrieve()` by injecting a large volume of tampered messages that slow down processing, causing the timeout to fire before all legitimate Hashgraph history is delivered, resulting in an incomplete and potentially manipulated history view?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `findByFilter(newFilter)` in `poll()` returns a `Stream<TopicMessage>` ordered only by `consensusTimestamp` with no secondary ordering on `sequenceNumber`, so that two tampered messages with identical `consensusTimestamp` values are returned in arbitrary order, corrupting the apparent Hashgraph message sequence?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `isComplete()` check `filter.getLimit() == total.get()` where both are `long` values, by injecting exactly `limit` tampered messages before the legitimate ones arrive, causing the retriever to declare completion after serving only falsified Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of `topicId` consistency validation in `poll()` so that a tampered `TopicMessage` returned by `findByFilter()` with a different `topicId` than requested is silently forwarded to the subscriber, causing cross-topic Hashgraph history contamination?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `RepeatSpec.create(r -> !context.isComplete(), context.getNumRepeats())` in `retrieve()` where `numRepeats = Long.MAX_VALUE` for throttled mode, so that by injecting an endless stream of tampered messages that always fill a full page, the retriever never completes and continuously serves manipulated Hashgraph history indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `pageSize` reset to `0L` at the start of each `poll()` call (`context.getPageSize().set(0L)`) so that if a tampered message causes an exception mid-stream, `pageSize` remains 0, causing `isComplete()` to return `true` and terminating history retrieval before all legitimate messages are delivered?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `poll()` uses `Flux.fromStream()` which is not thread-safe, so that concurrent polling cycles on the same `PollingContext` cause race conditions in stream consumption, resulting in duplicate or missing Hashgraph messages being delivered to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of `message` payload integrity checks in `poll()` so that a tampered `TopicMessage.message` byte array is served to subscribers as authentic Hashgraph consensus data, allowing arbitrary content injection into the perceived Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of `initialTransactionId` validation in `poll()` so that a tampered `TopicMessage` with a forged `initialTransactionId` is served as part of a chunked message sequence, causing the subscriber to reconstruct a falsified multi-chunk Hashgraph message?
